title: Doctor & Pharmacy
situation: You're at the huisarts (GP) or apotheek (pharmacy) explaining symptoms and getting help.

phrases.hindi: Ik heb pijn in mijn... buik / hoofd / keel / rug / borst / been
phrases.english: I have pain in my... stomach / head / throat / back / chest / leg
phrases.context: 'Ik heb pijn in mijn...' = the standard pain description formula. Body parts: 'buik' (belly/stomach), 'hoofd' (head), 'keel' (throat), 'rug' (back), 'borst' (chest), 'been' (leg), 'arm' (arm)
phrases.pronunciation: ik HEB PAYN in mayn... BOWK / HOHFT / KAYL / RUCH / BORST / BAYN

phrases.hindi: Ik voel me niet goed. Ik ben ziek. Ik heb koorts.
phrases.english: I don't feel well. I'm sick. I have a fever.
phrases.context: 'Ik voel me niet goed' = I don't feel well. 'Koorts' = fever. 'Ik heb het koud/warm' = I feel cold/warm. These are the first things a doctor will ask about.
phrases.pronunciation: ik FOOL me NEET CHOOT. ik BEN ZEEK. ik HEB KOHRTS.

phrases.hindi: Hoe lang heeft u dit al? Wanneer is het begonnen?
phrases.english: How long have you had this? When did it start?
phrases.context: What the doctor will ask. 'Hoe lang...al?' = how long...already? 'Begonnen' = started (past participle of 'beginnen'). Answer: 'Sinds gisteren' (since yesterday), 'Al drie dagen' (for three days already)
phrases.pronunciation: hoo LANG HAYFT u dit al? WAN-neer is het be-CHON-nen?

phrases.hindi: Ik ben allergisch voor... penicilline / noten / pollen
phrases.english: I'm allergic to... penicillin / nuts / pollen
phrases.context: 'Allergisch voor' = allergic to. IMPORTANT to communicate this clearly. 'Ik slik al medicijnen' = I already take medication. 'Mag ik de bijsluiter?' = may I have the package insert?
phrases.pronunciation: ik ben a-LER-chis VOOR... pen-i-si-LEE-ne / NOH-ten / POL-len

phrases.hindi: Kunt u een recept uitschrijven? Heeft u dit zonder recept?
phrases.english: Can you write a prescription? Do you have this without a prescription?
phrases.context: 'Recept uitschrijven' = to write a prescription ('uitschrijven' = separable). At pharmacy: 'Ik kom mijn medicijnen ophalen' (I'm coming to pick up my medication)
phrases.pronunciation: KUNT u uhn re-SEPT OWT-schray-ven? HAYFT u dit ZON-der re-SEPT?

phrases.hindi: Wanneer moet ik dit innemen? Hoeveel keer per dag?
phrases.english: When should I take this? How many times per day?
phrases.context: 'Innemen' = to take (medication), separable: 'neem in'. 'Per dag' = per day. Answer might be: 'Drie keer per dag, na het eten' (three times a day, after eating). 'Na het eten' = after eating.
phrases.pronunciation: WAN-neer MOOT ik dit IN-nay-men? HOO-feel KAYR per DACH?

phrases.hindi: Ik wil graag een afspraak maken. Wanneer kunt u mij ontvangen?
phrases.english: I'd like to make an appointment. When can you see me?
phrases.context: 'Afspraak maken' = to make an appointment. 'Ontvangen' = to receive/see (a patient). For the huisarts, you'll usually call the 'praktijk' (practice). 'Dringend' = urgent.
phrases.pronunciation: ik WIL CHAACH uhn AF-spraak MAA-ken. WAN-neer KUNT u may ont-VAN-chen?

phrases.hindi: Is het ernstig? Moet ik rust houden? Wanneer ben ik beter?
phrases.english: Is it serious? Do I need to rest? When will I be better?
phrases.context: 'Ernstig' = serious. 'Rust houden' = to rest (keep rest). 'Beter worden' = to get better. Natural follow-up questions after the doctor's diagnosis.
phrases.pronunciation: is het ERNS-tich? MOOT ik RUST HOW-den? WAN-neer BEN ik BAY-ter?

grammar_notes: Body part ownership: Dutch uses 'mijn' (my): 'mijn hoofd doet pijn' (my head hurts). Lit: 'my head does pain'
'Pijn doen' = to hurt: 'mijn keel doet pijn' (my throat hurts). Also 'ik heb pijn in mijn...' (I have pain in my...)
Duration with 'al': 'ik heb dit al drie dagen' = I've had this for three days (already). 'Al' + time period shows duration from then to now
Separable verbs at pharmacy: innemen (in|nemen), ophalen (op|halen), uitschrijven (uit|schrijven)
culture_notes: The 'huisarts' (GP/family doctor) is the first point of contact — you need a referral to see specialists. Register with a huisarts in your neighborhood ASAP
Pharmacies are 'apotheek' — your doctor's prescription is sent electronically. Show your ID/BSN (citizen service number)
Dutch healthcare is generally excellent but can feel abrupt — doctors are efficient and direct. Don't expect long explanations unless you ask
Emergency number: 112 (EU standard). Non-emergency doctor: call your huisarts or 'huisartsenpost' (after-hours GP service)

skill_breakdown.explanation: Key patterns: 'Ik heb pijn in mijn [body part]', 'Ik heb [symptom]', 'Ik voel me [adjective]'. Learn the key body parts and symptom words. Dutch doctors will appreciate your attempt at Dutch.

more_examples.english: I have a cold. (lit: I am cold-ened)
I cough a lot. (hoesten = to cough)

more_examples.hindi: Ik heb last van mijn maag.
more_examples.english: I'm having trouble with my stomach.

practice_prompt: Visiting a Dutch doctor or pharmacy — describing symptoms, asking about medication, and making appointments
